STYLE: clean vector illustration, crisp outlines, flat color fills, warm golden hour light

VIEWS.isometric: Strict orthographic isometric game-map view with a broad readable scene layout, roughly 45° rotation and 30° tilt from horizontal. No perspective projection, no vanishing points, no lens distortion, no depth-of-field camera look, and no distance-based scale falloff: objects of the same real size must remain the same drawn size anywhere in the frame, whether near the bottom or top. Elevated viewpoint, camera pulled back to show connected areas without turning the image into a field of tiny same-scale clutter. Use controlled hidden-object density: props are grouped into logical clusters with visible breathing room between them, clear foreground/background separation, and readable object boundaries for phone-screen scanning. The composition should feel like a designed mobile puzzle scene, with asymmetric organic paths and discovery moments, not a Where's Waldo-style sheet of dozens of densely packed micro-details.
VIEWS.side_2d: Flat 2D side-view composition at straight-on eye level, side-scroller game framing. Level camera, wide establishing shot showing the full scene width without shrinking important objects into same-scale clutter. Use controlled hidden-object density: props are grouped into logical clusters with visible breathing room between them, clear foreground/background separation, and readable object boundaries for phone-screen scanning. The composition should feel like a designed mobile puzzle scene rather than an edge-to-edge sheet of dense micro-details.

STYLES.pixelart: Chunky 16-bit pixel art in the style of a SNES-era hidden-object game asset. Limited 32-color palette with vibrant saturated fills, hard edges, visible pixel grid, no anti-aliasing, no gradient shading, no digital smoothing. Each object built from clearly readable pixel clusters with crisp 1-pixel outlines.
STYLES.flatvector: Hand-drawn isometric illustration for a polished mobile hidden-object brain teaser. Use clean dark outlines around major shapes, strong readable silhouettes, and solid warm flat color fills with at most one or two simple shade accents per object. Build a scene with controlled hidden-object density: enough props for discovery, but grouped into logical clusters with visible breathing room between clusters so the player's eye can rest. Hide targets through clever attention and context cues — peeking from behind furniture, sitting beside matching objects, partially occluded by a clear foreground prop — not through blur, noise, camouflage sludge, or eye-fatiguing micro-detail. Every important object should have a distinct boundary and readable local contrast on a phone screen. Prefer simple geometric props, clear edges, intentional negative space, and logical placement paths that make the scene feel designed rather than randomly cluttered. Avoid sfumato, blended/melted edges, ambiguous merged objects, photorealistic texture noise, dense repeated tiny patterns, excessive wood grain, over-detailed surface scratches, muddy gradients, and low-contrast object boundaries. Warm muted palette — amber, terracotta, dusty rose, pale cream, deep teal shadows — with consistent outline weight across the scene. The result should sit between Find The Cat 2, Categoria, and Scavenger Hunt: polished and customized, playful but refined, never generic AI slop.
STYLES.clean_old_cartoon: Vintage hand-drawn cartoon background adapted for a modern mobile puzzle game. Rounded old-cartoon shapes, cheerful simplified silhouettes, thick confident ink outlines, flat poster-like color fills, and soft hand-painted background texture. Use broad readable color masses and minimal internal linework so the scene stays clear on a phone screen. Charming and slightly nostalgic, clean and polished for mobile, with less visual noise, fewer texture marks, and no dense rendering.
STYLES.watercolor: Loose watercolor painting in the style of Beatrix Potter children's book illustration. Wet-on-wet bleeds where pigment pools at the edges, visible cold-press paper texture, translucent washes layered over a faint pencil under-drawing. Soft edges, muted earthy palette, no hard outlines, no digital sharpness.
STYLES.comic: Bold western comic book illustration in the style of 1970s Marvel splash pages. Heavy black ink outlines with varying line weight, Ben-Day halftone dot shading, dynamic foreshortening, four-color saturated palette (cyan, magenta, yellow, black). Visible newsprint-process texture, no gradients, no airbrushed lighting.
STYLES.lineart: Black ink line drawing on pure white background, in the style of an adult coloring book page. Uniform clean outlines, varying line weight only at the deepest shadows, sparse cross-hatching for shadow regions. No color fills, no grayscale, no gradients. Hand-drawn feel with confident strokes, generous white space inside each shape.
STYLES.where_is_waldo: Wimmelbilder hidden-object illustration in the style of Martin Handford's Where's Wally / Where's Waldo books. Thin, slightly jittery hand-drawn black ink outlines. Flat-tint vintage 1980s color washes (bright primary reds, yellows, blues) applied over the ink. No gradients, no 3D shading, no digital glows. Extreme visual density — every square inch packed with dozens of tiny non-repeating characters performing distinct funny micro-interactions, no single dominant subject, eye travels across the whole frame.
STYLES.old_pixel_art: Chunky pixel art, retro 16-bit game aesthetic, vibrant saturated colors, hard edges, no anti-aliasing

scenes.japan_morning_market: A Japanese village fish market sprawling across multiple connected sections — a main lane with stalls, a side alley branching off, a canal-side strip with more stalls along the water. The entire ground surface is consistent cobblestone paving throughout — every area the viewer can see the ground, it reads as stone pavers with no untextured blank areas. Wooden stalls of varying sizes crowd unevenly, some clustered tightly, others standing alone with gaps. Tiled rooftops at different heights, some overlapping, some freestanding. A narrow canal cuts through at an unexpected angle with one small arched bridge. Scattered throughout without pattern: crates of fish, barrels, woven baskets, hanging paper lanterns glowing soft amber, food carts with steam plumes, stacked crates, coiled ropes, sake barrels, hanging noren curtains, wooden signs, umbrellas, lantern poles, fish baskets, stone lanterns, potted plants, wooden buckets, rope bundles. Dense clusters in some areas, sparser breathing room in others. Asymmetric, organic layout — like a real village that grew over time.

ENTITY_PROMPT_TEMPLATE: Add exactly one {entity} to this crop. Place exactly one {entity} centered on the marked target area — do not repeat the subject. Match the scene's illustration style, line weight, palette, and flat-shaded rendering, but keep the {entity}'s silhouette and boundary readable on a phone screen. The {entity} may be partially occluded or contextually disguised, but must not disappear through muddy edges, low contrast, texture camouflage, or ambiguous merged shapes. When the {entity} is a dog, vary breed, coat color, markings, ear shape, tail shape, and body type across crops so the level does not repeat the same dog design. The {entity} is a charming little anthropomorphic inhabitant of this world — a character with personality going about its business. Give it something simple and specific to do with its surroundings: holding an object, using a tool, sitting at a small task, peeking at something, carrying something, watching something with interest, tending a small thing, pausing mid-task. Keep the pose clear and readable. The charm comes from the context and the expression, not from dynamic motion. Size the {entity} realistically relative to the scene around it — compare it to whatever human-scale objects are visible in the crop and render the {entity} at the physical size a real {entity} would be next to them. Not so small that its species is ambiguous, not so large that it dominates the crop. The {entity} must be fully contained within the visible frame. Keep all other elements of the image unchanged.
_OLD_PIXEL_ART_ENTITY_PROMPT_TEMPLATE: Replace the center of this image with a {entity}. 8-bit pixel sprite style, flat colors, black outline, no anti-aliasing. The {entity} should be blending in to the background. It can be doing something related to the background. If there is a chair sitting on it, if there is a window leaning outside of it. Preserve the background exactly.
VARIATION_SYSTEM_PROMPT:
You are a scene description generator for a hidden-object puzzle game called 'Find the Dog'. Given a general scene description (content, perspective, art style), generate a SPECIFIC, UNIQUE variation of that scene with concrete details — specific objects, colors, arrangements, landmarks, and atmospheric details that make this particular level distinct from others with the same theme. Do NOT mention dogs or animals — they will be added separately. Output EXACTLY two lines in this format (no preamble, no extra lines, no markdown):
TITLE: <2-4 word evocative title in Title Case>
SCENE: <2-3 sentence scene description>
LANDSCAPE_PANORAMIC_PREFIX: Wide panoramic 16:9 widescreen composition designed for a horizontally-panning camera. The scene extends across three distinct visual sections from left to right — a left zone with its own anchoring feature, a central zone with a hinge feature, a right zone with its own anchoring feature. A continuous horizontal spine runs across all three sections — a path, canal, river, road, shoreline, or architectural line — so the image reads as one continuous place, not three separate panels. Composition distributes visual weight evenly across the full width, with density and interest spread left-to-right rather than concentrated at the center. The top and bottom strips of the frame stay visually simpler, keeping critical detail in the middle band where gameplay happens.
